beats.reasoning: Rainy morning and her homebody routine fit a slower start with obsessive ferry schedule checking.

beats.reasoning: The SR-520 eastbound closure 9am–noon likely forces earlier departure and extra buffer while she reaches her job site.

beats.reasoning: Deep-focus morning work matches her tax-season pattern and keeps her indoors during steady rain.

beats.reasoning: With rain from 7am–2pm, a quick indoor meal fits and avoids unnecessary walking.

diary:
Rain started before I even got my tea going, the kind of steady, grey drizzle that makes the world feel quieter. I did a quick tidy in Kirkland, checked ferry/weather updates like they were part of my morning routine, and tried to convince my brain it was “tax season time” even though my hoodie still smelled faintly like last weekend’s trails. The SR-520 closure hit me right in the commute—Redmond Main Campus looked the same, but the streets felt slower, like everyone was bracing for the same inconvenience.

Work at Building 1 was spreadsheets and reconciliations all morning, then I grabbed a warm lunch at QFC in Issaquah and ate at my desk just to dodge the drizzle. I stole a little reset at Liberty Park in Bellevue, then came back to closing entries. Volunteering at Food Lifeline in the evening steadied me, and later I knitted at home while checking ferry times for a future hike. I feel tired, but solid—like the day actually landed.
thought_process: I leaned into routines today because the weather and the SR-520 closure were already noisy enough. Morning tea, tidy, and weather/ferry checks were my way of keeping uncertainty from spilling into my work. I chose to eat lunch inside at QFC because rain changes my tempo—outdoors makes me feel slower, so staying put kept my focus sharp for the reconciliations. At work, I pushed through the detail-heavy tasks first, since those are the ones that get harder when my patience thins. The sheltered break at Liberty Park wasn’t just comfort; it was a mental recalibration. The rain could’ve made me grumpy, but volunteering at Food Lifeline shifted my mood—suddenly numbers weren’t the only thing that mattered. By evening, I felt calmer and more hopeful about the next hike.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
